issues.details: sample-scrape.json names the benefits pattern "benefitsSection" but block-inventory.json registers it as "benefitsGrid". Functionally identical (same AEM component: title+text in container, same columns block mapping), but the rename means a naive string-match check fails to confirm 100% coverage of the 12 scrape patterns.
issues.remediation: Align the pattern name in block-inventory.json to "benefitsSection" to match sample-scrape.json, or add a mapping alias so automated coverage checks pass cleanly.

issues.details: summary.fromBlockCollection = 6 but blockPaletteSummary.blocksFromCollection lists 7 entries (hero, accordion, cards, carousel, columns, tabs, fragment). The fragment block is in the palette but excluded from the content-pattern count — this should be explicitly noted in the summary.
issues.remediation: Update summary.fromBlockCollection to 7, or add a note explaining that fragment is an infrastructure block not counted as a content-mapping block.

issues.details: block-inventory.json has 13 contentPatternMapping entries (adds "topicMenu") vs the 12 content patterns in sample-scrape.json. topicMenu is a legitimate addition found during inventory, but its origin is not explained — was it found in a page not included in the 12-pattern sample?
issues.remediation: Add a note explaining topicMenu was discovered from the nav structure / local block analysis, not from the initial 12-pattern sample scrape.

issues.details: chrome/footer.html (299 bytes) contains only an empty AEM container div with no rendered links or content. chrome/footer.links.json is []. While documented in README.md ("Footer: Empty in AEM template"), this means downstream phases have no footer link reference data. The actual footer content (nav groups, legal links, social links) is in the page body, not the <footer> element.
issues.remediation: Consider supplementing chrome/footer.links.json with links extracted from the page-body footer area (the accordion/nav groups visible at the bottom of pages), since the EDS footer fragment will need those links.

evidence.finding: manifest.json contains 14 pages (totalPages: 14, pages array length: 14). Exceeds minimum of 10. Archetypes: homepage (2), treatment-page (5), faq (1), clinic-finder (3), legal (2), contact (1).
All 3 randomly sampled URLs returned HTTP 200 with substantial content (118K, 216K, 102K bytes respectively). No auth walls or error pages.
Sitemap has 13 URLs; manifest has 14 pages. Manifest has MORE pages than sitemap (crawler captured the root / in addition to sitemap URLs, plus 3 clinic variants). No crawl truncation.

Archetypes are sensible: homepage (juvederm.nl, juvederm.nl/nl), treatment-page (5 treatment areas), faq (/nl/qa), clinic-finder (3 clinic search pages), legal (disclaimer + terms), contact. All nav header links map to discovered archetypes.

14 page directories present under source-bundle/pages/. Each contains desktop.png, mobile.png, index.html, meta.json. chrome/ contains header.html (43KB), footer.html (299B), header.links.json (27 links), footer.links.json (empty - documented site issue), header-desktop.png, footer-desktop.png.

header.html is 43078 bytes with full AEM EMU navigation markup. header.links.json has 27 links including logo, 5 treatment nav items, FAQ sublinks, and clinic-finder CTA. footer.html is 299 bytes (empty AEM container) — this is a documented site-level issue, not a capture failure. README explicitly explains the AEM footer element contains no rendered content.
evidence.note: footer.links.json is [] — downstream EDS footer fragment will need links sourced from page body.

evidence.finding: All 14 page slugs have both desktop.png and mobile.png. Chrome has header-desktop.png and footer-desktop.png. No missing screenshots.
evidence.note: No migrated preview URLs exist at this phase (crawler phase precedes migration). Screenshot pair comparison against preview is N/A for this phase.

evidence.finding: README documents: Playwright Chromium headless; desktop 1440x900 + mobile 390x844; waitUntil domcontentloaded + networkidle (12s timeout); content-stability loop (500ms samples x4, main.innerText.length stable > 50 chars); OneTrust dismiss via #onetrust-accept-btn-handler; 800ms settle before screenshot; separate browser context per viewport.

homepage/index.html (389KB) yields 11,425 chars of visible text. nl/index.html (266KB): 8,036 chars. nl-treatment-lips/index.html (350KB): 13,159 chars. nl-qa/index.html (346KB): 24,165 chars. All are well above 50-char threshold and consistent with fully hydrated AEM pages.

block-inventory.json is valid JSON. Contains: summary, localBlocks (13 local blocks), blockCollectionAvailable, contentPatternMapping (13 entries), blockPaletteSummary, reusabilityAnalysis, archetypeBlockUsage, implementationPriority.

11 of 12 sample-scrape patterns are explicitly covered by name. "benefitsSection" is covered but renamed "benefitsGrid" in the inventory — functionally identical mapping (same AEM component, same EDS block: columns). Inventory adds "topicMenu" as a 13th pattern from local block discovery.

comparisonSlider -> before-after (custom, medium complexity, drag+carousel+touch); faqAccordion -> accordion (block-collection); treatmentCardsCarousel -> carousel (block-collection). All three key interactive patterns are covered with appropriate block decisions.

Summary claims 6 BC + 4 custom. Palette lists 7 BC (including fragment) + 4 custom (before-after, clinic-finder, topic-menu, metadata). Discrepancy because fragment is infrastructure, not a content-mapping block. The 4 custom blocks (before-after, clinic-finder, topic-menu, plus metadata/embed handling) are reasonable — no Block Collection equivalent for comparison slider, clinic-finder map integration, or treatment topic nav.

pharmaNotice -> default-content (required pharma disclaimer, simpler as styled prose). referenceSection -> default-content (legal/footnote references, no interactive elements). scrollReveal -> css-only (animation-only, no authoring need). All three decisions are sound EDS practice.

reusabilityAnalysis.sharedSections documents: treatment-tabs (fragment recommendation, shared across homepage + all treatment pages), clinic-finder-cta (fragment for compact variant), pharma-notice (fragment recommendation, identical on all pages). All three fragments from the orchestrator brief are documented with path and usage scope.

summary: PASS — Crawler delivered 14-page manifest (>10 minimum), source bundle is fully formed with desktop+mobile screenshots and post-hydration HTML for all pages, render strategy is documented, and block inventory covers all 12 content patterns (benefitsSection->benefitsGrid rename is a low-severity naming inconsistency). Four low-severity issues noted: pattern rename, block count summary discrepancy, undocumented topicMenu origin, and empty footer chrome (a documented site-level AEM issue).
